summary: Core E2E lane, authenticated test-login refresh, optional profile split, and stale public/detail/TOC test assumptions were stabilized.
changed: Added ensureAdminSession(page, returnPath?) and ensureAdminApiContext(request, returnPath?).
Refreshed admin sessions for chromium-authenticated Playwright tests.
Changed content fixtures to use an authenticated admin API context and public visibility polling.
Added PLAYWRIGHT_E2E_PROFILE core, optional, and exhaustive behavior.
Updated package scripts for test:e2e, test:e2e:optional, and test:e2e:exhaustive.
Updated public detail title and TOC test IDs.
Updated stale Playwright selectors and mutable content assumptions.
intentionallyNotChanged: No broadening of test-login for real public production use.
No deletion of optional recording/live/batch/manual-heavy specs.
No reverting unrelated dirty worktree changes.

targeted: Public detail/TOC passed on fresh port 3001 after cold-route rerun.
Works video targeted group passed after one transient direct rerun.

risks: The final full core run used a fresh local dev endpoint rather than a rebuilt Docker production-like endpoint.
Existing Docker production-like containers predated the final edits and were not used as the final validation source.
Five existing Playwright tests remain intentionally skipped in core.
Post-audit correction: an existing nginx container exposed host ports 80 and 443, which does not match the requested Docker test boundary. It was stopped after review.
recommendation: Use npm run test:e2e as the release-blocking core signal, then rebuild the Docker test stack bound only to http://127.0.0.1:3000 and rerun the core lane before production promotion.
